text.title: Embedding Provider Examples
text.summary: Code examples for OpenAI, local SentenceTransformers, Ollama, and Cohere embedding providers.

section.title: Provider Comparison
section.p: Choose provider based on: privacy (local), cost (OpenAI small / Cohere), quality (OpenAI large / BGE-M3), multilingual (BGE-M3 / multilingual-MiniLM), or zero-infra (Ollama local).

example.code:
from openai import OpenAI import numpy as np
client = OpenAI()
def get_embedding(text: str, model: str = "text-embedding-3-small") -> list[float]: text = text.replace("\n", " ") response = client.embeddings.create(input=text, model=model) return response.data[0].embedding
def get_embeddings_batch( texts: list[str], model: str = "text-embedding-3-small", batch_size: int = 2048, ) -> list[list[float]]: texts = [t.replace("\n", " ") for t in texts] all_embeddings: list[list[float]] = [] for i in range(0, len(texts), batch_size): batch = texts[i : i + batch_size] response = client.embeddings.create(input=batch, model=model) sorted_data = sorted(response.data, key=lambda x: x.index) all_embeddings.extend([e.embedding for e in sorted_data]) return all_embeddings
# Dimension reduction (text-embedding-3 only) def get_reduced_embedding(text: str, dimensions: int = 256) -> list[float]: response = client.embeddings.create( input=text, model="text-embedding-3-small", dimensions=dimensions ) return response.data[0].embedding
example.note: Sort by index is mandatory for batch calls; the API may return items out of order.
BGE-M3 sparse output is dict[token → weight], not a dense array; needs adaptation before storing in most vector DBs.
For large corpora use a local SentenceTransformer instead; Ollama's per-call overhead makes it 10-50x slower for batch work.

example.code:
import cohere
co = cohere.Client(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")
def get_cohere_embeddings( texts: list[str], input_type: str = "search_document", # or "search_query" ) -> list[list[float]]: """ input_type is mandatory for v3 models. - "search_document": use when indexing - "search_query": use when embedding queries Mixing types degrades retrieval by ~5-10% NDCG. """ response = co.embed( texts=texts, model="embed-english-v3.0", input_type=input_type, truncate="END", ) return response.embeddings
example.note: Always pass input_type; omitting it defaults to a generic mode that underperforms asymmetric retrieval.
